1	pi-blaster
2	==========
3	
4	This project enables PWM on the GPIO pins you request of a Raspberry Pi. The
5	technique used is extremely efficient: does not use the CPU and gives very
6	stable pulses.
7	
8	Pi-blaster project is based on the excellent work of Richard Hirst for
9	[ServoBlaster](https://github.com/richardghirst/PiBits).
10

Mama mia, what a codebase! Now I've got the full picture. Let me make my pick and explain it!

---

## 🔧 Issue Chosen: **#66 — "Support GPIO pins above 31"** (also affects #122, #114 crashers on Compute Modules)

**Why this one?**

It's the repo owner's own bug report, it's a clear, demonstrable C code bug (not a "works on my hardware" mystery), and it affects multiple downstream issues. Here's exactly what's broken:

| Location | Bug |
|---|---|
| `gpio_set()` | Uses `1 << pin` — **undefined behaviour in C** for `pin >= 32` (shifting a 32-bit int by 32+). Also always writes to `GPIO_SET0`/`GPIO_CLR0` (pins 0–31 only) |
| `update_pwm()` | Same `1 << pin2gpio[i]` UB; only builds one 32-bit mask for one GPIO register |
| `init_ctrl_data()` | Same mask UB; initialises only the lower GPIO bank |
| `parseargs()` | Hard-rejects any pin `>= MAX_CHANNELS (32)` — no pins 32–53 ever allowed |
| `is_banned_pin()` | Loops up to `num_channels` instead of the actual `banned_pins[]` array size — could silently miss banned pins |

**The fix plan:** Add a second GPIO bank (`GPIO_SET1`/`GPIO_CLR1` for pins 32–53), a second DMA sample array, and change `NUM_CBS` from `N*2` to `N*3` (GPIO-bank-0-write → GPIO-bank-1-write → PWM-pace per time slot). Clean, systematic, no logic restructuring needed.

Let's unclog these pipes! 🪠
